title: Security defined
doc_html:
Art. 3136. Security defined

            Security is an accessory right established by legislation or contract over property, or an obligation undertaken by a person other than the principal obligor, to secure performance of an obligation. It is accessory to the obligation it secures and is transferred with the obligation without a special provision to that effect.

            Acts 2014, No. 281, §1, eff. Jan. 1, 2015.
doc_text:
Art. 3136.  Security defined
Security is an accessory right established by legislation or contract over property, or
an obligation undertaken by a person other than the principal obligor, to secure performance
of an obligation. It is accessory to the obligation it secures and is transferred with the
obligation without a special provision to that effect.
Acts 2014, No. 281, §1, eff. Jan. 1, 2015.
